strengths: Cloud data platform experience (BigQuery, Snowflake, GCS)
weaknesses: Unclear years in software-engineer title (roles mixed)
improvement_bullets: Developed cloud-native data pipelines on AWS using serverless services (Lambda/S3), orchestrating file ingestion and transformation of CSV and Parquet formats to optimize downstream data availability.
Built and maintained internal operational dashboards using React, providing stakeholders with real-time visibility into data quality metrics and pipeline health.
Engineered robust data observability solutions that track schema evolution and provide automated alerting, ensuring consistent data integrity across complex distributed systems.
Implemented 'human-in-the-loop' audit trails within data processing workflows, allowing for manual verification of AI-driven model outputs to ensure high confidence and explainability.
Enhanced backend API architectures to include comprehensive automated testing and schema validation, significantly reducing production bugs during high-volume data ingestion.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Software Engineer III opportunity with Verisk. I am interested in this opportunity because of Verisk's commitment to translating big data into actionable insights for the global insurance industry, a mission that aligns perfectly with my background in data engineering and backend development. What interests me about working for Verisk is its reputation as a leading data analytics partner and its clear investment in an AI-first approach to solving complex risk modeling challenges. I believe my expertise in building scalable ELT pipelines and my proficiency in Python and cloud-based data architecture would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by prioritizing automation and data quality as the foundation of reliable software. In fact, at my previous role, I designed a production-grade ELT pipeline that ingested high-volume economic data using Airflow, dbt, and BigQuery, while implementing automated testing via Great Expectations. This initiative resulted in a 40% improvement in operational efficiency and significantly reduced query times for stakeholders. Ultimately, I know that the Software Engineer III role at Verisk would allow me to bring my experience in building resilient, observable data pipelines to the design and implementation of modern AI-first ingestion services. Additionally, my role as a Full-Stack Software Developer at Haven Technologies enabled me to develop my skills in building RESTful APIs and collaborating cross-functionally to drive feature adoption. I worked closely with product managers to translate complex user feedback into clean, performant code, which directly contributed to a 15% increase in feature usage and improved data accuracy by 60%. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in cloud infrastructure and software best practices in working on the next generation of file processing systems at Verisk. I know that my proficiency in Python/SQL and my commitment to data observability align with what it takes to succeed at the Software Engineer III role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
